assertion_results.text: The plan references inspecting existing code before modifying it — mentions reading or analyzing at least 2 of: advisory/endpoints/get.rs, advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/model/summary.rs, common/src/error.rs (constraint 1.5)
assertion_results.evidence: The conventions.md references analyzing sibling code patterns from multiple files. It explicitly mentions: 'All endpoint handlers in modules/fundamental/src/*/endpoints/' (covering get.rs), 'AppError enum in common/src/error.rs' (covering error.rs), and service method patterns from advisory/service. The file-2-description.md states 'follows the exact pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/get.rs'. The file-4-description.md references patterns from 'fetch' and 'list' methods in advisory/service/advisory.rs. At least 3 of the 4 listed files are referenced as being inspected.

assertion_results.text: All files listed in the plan are within the scope defined by the task's Files to Modify and Files to Create sections — no unrelated files are modified (constraint 1.4, 5.1)
assertion_results.evidence: The plan.md lists exactly 3 files to create (severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, advisory_summary.rs test file) and 3 files to modify (advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, advisory/model/mod.rs). The plan explicitly states under 'No Changes Needed': 'server/src/main.rs — routes auto-mount via module registration (confirmed by task description)'. No unrelated files are listed for modification. The 6 file description files (file-1 through file-6) correspond exactly to these 6 files.

assertion_results.text: The plan includes creating the 3 new files listed in Files to Create: severity_summary.rs model, severity_summary.rs endpoint handler, and advisory_summary.rs test file
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Files to Create' section lists: 1. 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/severity_summary.rs' (model), 2. 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/severity_summary.rs' (endpoint handler), 3. 'tests/api/advisory_summary.rs' (integration tests). Corresponding detailed descriptions are in file-1-description.md (CREATE), file-2-description.md (CREATE), and file-3-description.md (CREATE).

assertion_results.text: The plan includes modifying the 3 files listed in Files to Modify: advisory/service/advisory.rs, advisory/endpoints/mod.rs, and advisory/model/mod.rs
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md 'Files to Modify' section lists: 4. 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/service/advisory.rs', 5. 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/mod.rs', 6. 'modules/fundamental/src/advisory/model/mod.rs'. Corresponding detailed descriptions are in file-4-description.md (MODIFY), file-5-description.md (MODIFY), and file-6-description.md (MODIFY).

assertion_results.text: The planned commit message follows Conventional Commits format (type(scope): description) and references TC-9201 in the footer (constraint 2.1, 2.2)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md contains the commit message: 'feat(api): add advisory severity aggregation endpoint' with body text and footer 'Implements TC-9201'. This follows Conventional Commits format with type 'feat', scope '(api)', and description. TC-9201 is referenced in the footer.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions using --trailer='Assisted-by: Claude Code' in the commit (constraint 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 41 states: 'The commit would include --trailer="Assisted-by: Claude Code".' This explicitly references the trailer requirement.

assertion_results.text: The plan references creating a branch named TC-9201 or notes the branch naming convention (constraint 3.1), and references the Target Branch value from the task description
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md lines 9-10 state: 'Branch name: TC-9201' and 'Base branch: main'. This satisfies both the branch naming convention (using the Jira ticket ID) and references the target branch value 'main'.

assertion_results.text: The conventions analysis identifies at least 2 patterns from sibling code: error handling with Result<T, AppError> and .context(), and the model/service/endpoints module structure
assertion_results.evidence: conventions.md explicitly identifies both patterns: Under 'Module structure': 'Each domain module follows a strict model/ + service/ + endpoints/ directory structure.' Under 'Error handling': 'All errors use AppError from common/src/error.rs. Errors are wrapped with .context("descriptive message") for traceability.' Under 'Endpoint patterns': 'All handlers return Result<T, AppError> with .context() wrapping for error propagation.' Both patterns are clearly documented.

assertion_results.text: The plan extracts and references the Target Branch section from the task description, identifying 'main' as the target branch
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 10 states 'Base branch: main'. This identifies 'main' as the target/base branch.

assertion_results.text: The plan mentions checking for a description digest comment (Step 1.5) and notes that when no digest is found, it proceeds with a warning rather than blocking execution (backward compatibility per shared/description-digest-protocol.md)
assertion_results.evidence: None of the output files (plan.md, conventions.md, or any file-*-description.md) mention a description digest comment, Step 1.5 digest checking, or any backward compatibility behavior regarding digest validation. The plan does not reference this protocol at all.
